roleGuard.description: You need administrator privileges to access this page.

projectDialog.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the <name>{name}</name> project? This action cannot be undone.

validation.name: Please enter a name
validation.slugPattern: Only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens are allowed
validation.passwordMin: Password must be at least 8 characters

generic.requestError: An error occurred while processing your request

generic.sessionDeleteError: An error occurred while deleting the session.

solutions.sessionMissing: Session info missing. Please restart from the beginning.
solutions.companyNameMissing: Company name is missing. Please go back to step 1 and enter the company name.
solutions.sessionExpired: Session expired. Please sign in again.

pmRecommend.analyzing: Analyzing the best PM for your prototype
pmRecommend.nextHint: Click the Next button below to select a PM.
pmRecommend.failDesc: A temporary error occurred. Please try again.

pmSelect.loadingDesc: Searching for PM profiles optimized for your prototype
pmSelect.recommendBanner: AI has recommended the best PM based on your selected prototype. Higher match rates indicate better project success probability.

pmComposition.nextHint: This PM's default team (agents, skills, MCP, hooks) will be automatically selected in the next agent configuration step. PM team items are locked and cannot be removed, but you can freely add more items.

agents.pmLockBanner: {pmName}'s default team items have been automatically applied. Locked items cannot be removed, but you can freely add additional items.
agents.catalogRecBanner: Agents and skills have been recommended based on your selected prototype catalog. Adjust as needed.
agents.defaultBanner: Review the agent configuration for your selected prototype and adjust as needed.
agents.ticketDesc: Select the platform to manage issues/tickets.
agents.ticketLockedByPm: {pmName} has locked {label}. Other ticket sources cannot be selected.
agents.ticketRequiredAlert: Please select 1 ticket source (Linear or Notion)

platform.description: Select the Agent platform to run your generated solution.
platform.comingSoonNote: Gemini CLI, Cursor, and Codex support will be available in future updates.
platform.claudeCodeDesc: AI coding agent based on Anthropic Claude
platform.geminiCliDesc: CLI agent based on Google Gemini

os.description: Select the OS environment to unzip and run the project. A launcher script for your selected environment will be included.
os.wslGuideDesc: WSL2 is Microsoft's official feature for running a Linux environment on Windows.
os.wslStep2: After restarting your PC, open the Ubuntu app
os.wslStep3: Set username and password, then you're done
os.wsl2Desc: Windows Subsystem for Linux 2 — Claude Code officially recommended environment

env.authApiKeyDesc: Enter the sk-ant-... key issued from Anthropic Console directly.
env.authOAuthDesc: For Claude Pro/Max subscribers only. When you run bash start.sh, claude login will proceed automatically.
env.oauthNote: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY will not be included in .env. Browser login will start automatically when you run bash start.sh.
env.nonAsciiWarning: Non-ASCII characters (Korean, emoji, etc.) are automatically removed. Enter only English letters and numbers.
env.missingKeysAlert: {count} required key(s) not yet configured. Enter values or mark as "Enter later".
env.deferredKeysNote: {count} key(s) to enter later — you can add them in the final confirmation step.
env.trackingDesc: Select how to automatically trigger local Claude when Linear issue status changes.
env.cfNote: Run bash scripts/setup-tunnel.sh after unzipping to auto-install cloudflared.
env.pollingNote: Run python3 scripts/linear_watcher.py to poll Linear every 30 seconds. No webhook server needed.
env.securityNote: The keys entered are saved in .env in the ZIP file. Do not share the ZIP file. Refer to the docs/api-keys/ folder in the ZIP if unsure.
env.securityNoteOAuth: In browser OAuth mode, the Anthropic key is not included in .env. claude login will start automatically when you run bash start.sh.

roi.title: ROI Comparison
roi.desc: Compare the cost of building the same solution with traditional staffing vs. ClickEye.
roi.rateNote: * Adjusting rates does not change the administrator's standard values. Only applies to this session. Formula version: {version}

simpleConfirm.submitHint: Creating a project will save your solution configuration

confirmation.deferredDesc: These are keys you chose to enter later. Enter them now or edit the .env file directly after creating the project.

prototypeCard.aiMatchNote: AI analysis text is currently shown in Korean. Backend i18n in progress.

companyDescription.label: Company description
companyDescription.placeholder: Describe your company background, team structure, tech level, etc.

solutionRequest.label: Describe the solution you need
solutionRequest.hint: Write freely in natural language. AI will analyze and design the optimal solution.
solutionRequest.placeholder: e.g. We want to automate our customer data management currently handled in Excel. We need a CRM system for 10 sales team members, ideally integrated with Slack...

autoDecompose.title: ClickEye Auto Analysis
autoDecompose.description: When ON, running bash start.sh after download will automatically analyze and decompose solution requirements to generate AI Team review cards. Turn OFF to design manually.

step2.waitingHint: This usually takes 30 seconds to 1 minute. Please wait.
step2.doneHint: Click the Next button below to review the prototypes.

disabled.title: Live preview unavailable
disabled.description: The live preview feature is currently disabled.
disabled.contactAdmin: Please contact an administrator to enable this feature.

failed.title: Prototype generation failed
failed.description: A temporary error occurred. Please try again.

empty.title: No prototypes generated
empty.description: Please go back and review the information.

step3.aiHint: AI-analyzed solution candidates. Select the most suitable direction.

setupGuide.title: Solution created!
setupGuide.subtitle: Follow the steps below to set up your local development environment
setupGuide.workflowDesc1: bash start.sh → Environment check · Background service start · Auto exit
setupGuide.workflowDesc2Wsl: bash scripts/install-service.sh → Register systemd service (auto-recover after WSL2 restart)
setupGuide.workflowDesc3Wsl: ClickEye Web → AI Team → Approve task (Todo)
setupGuide.workflowDesc3: ClickEye Web → AI Team → Approve task (Todo)
setupGuide.workflowDesc4Wsl: Local pipeline automatically calls Claude to write code
setupGuide.workflowDesc4: Local pipeline automatically calls Claude to write code
setupGuide.workflowSlide: Team sharing slide guide: docs/setup-guide.pptx in ZIP
setupGuide.projectPageHint: Download ZIP and check integration status on the project page
setupGuide.step1Desc: Click the "Download ZIP" button on the project page
setupGuide.step2DescWsl: Unzip to your desired folder in WSL2 Ubuntu terminal
setupGuide.step2Desc: Unzip the ZIP file to your desired folder
setupGuide.step2NoteWsl: Run from the WSL terminal instead of double-clicking the ZIP in Windows Explorer
setupGuide.step3Desc: Checks environment, starts services in the background, then exits automatically. The pipeline continues running even after closing the terminal.
setupGuide.step4DescWsl: Services will automatically recover after WSL2 shutdown or restart. Register as a systemd user service.
setupGuide.step4Desc: Once ClickEye Web AI Team registers a task, the local pipeline will run automatically.
setupGuide.step4NoteWsl: Optional but recommended for stable operation

preset.title: Quick Start
preset.subtitle: Select a preset to automatically configure agents, skills, and pipelines
preset.presetsEmpty: No presets available. Configure manually in the wizard.

maturity.title: Maturity Assessment
maturity.subtitle: Assess your team's development maturity and get preset recommendations
maturity.resultSubtitle: We've analyzed your team's AI development maturity
maturity.questionsError: Unable to load questions. Please try again later.

naturalLanguage.title: Configure with natural language
naturalLanguage.subtitle: Describe your project in natural language to get optimal configuration recommendations
naturalLanguage.placeholder: e.g. I want to build a SaaS with Next.js + FastAPI. I need code review and test automation.

team.label: Team Capability
team.description: Tell us about your team size and AI experience level

process.label: Development Process
process.description: We check your code review and branching strategy

tooling.label: Development Tools
tooling.description: We assess your IDE and test automation level

ci.label: CI/CD
ci.description: We check your pipeline and deployment frequency

ai.label: AI Utilization
ai.description: We evaluate your AI tool usage and quality management

page.title: Solution Wizard
page.subtitle: AI automatically designs solutions tailored to your company
page.heroTitle: Design AI solutions with a 7-step wizard
page.heroDesc: Enter your company info and AI will generate a custom prototype, then guide you through PM recommendations, agent configuration, and platform selection.

new.resumeDesc: You can continue a previous wizard session or start a new one.

page.wizardBannerDesc: Design an AI solution yourself and get started with a ZIP download
page.modernizeBannerDesc: Connect a GitHub repository and AI will diagnose and automatically register modernization tasks in Linear
page.title: Projects
page.subtitle: Manage projects for AI agents to work on

detail.staleKeyDesc: Your local .env file needs to be updated. {keys} key(s) are outdated.
detail.noWizardData: No wizard configuration available. Run the solution wizard again to regenerate the ZIP.
detail.oauthNote: OAuth browser auth mode. claude login will proceed automatically when you run bash start.sh.
detail.envNote: API keys are not stored on the server. They are written directly to the .env in the ZIP.
detail.missingEnvAlert: {count} required environment variable(s) ({labels}) must be entered to download the ZIP.
detail.downloadHintReady: ZIP will be generated with saved config + your API keys
detail.sanitizedWarning: Non-ASCII characters (Korean, emoji, etc.) are automatically removed. Enter only letters and numbers.
detail.dangerZoneDesc: Actions below cannot be undone. Proceed with caution.
detail.resetDesc: Deletes wizard configuration, tickets, artifacts, and session data. Project ID and name are preserved.
detail.deleteDesc: Permanently deletes the project and all associated data.
detail.resetKeyUpdateNote: Update LICENSE_KEY in your local .env file with the new key above.

linear.credentialsNotSavedDesc: Enter Linear API key and team ID when creating a project to auto-save
linear.savedNote: Linear credentials are saved for this project.
linear.notSavedNote: Enter your Linear API key in the project creation wizard to save it for this project.

members.title: Organization Members
members.subtitle: Invite and manage organization members
members.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove?
members.emptyStateHint: Use the 'Invite member' button above to add members

linear.title: Linear Integration
linear.subtitle: AI Team tasks are automatically registered as Linear issues, and local Claude runs automatically when status changes.
linear.apiKeyHelp: Issued from Linear → Settings → API → Personal API keys
linear.teamIdHelp: Linear → Settings → Workspace → Teams → Click the team name → Copy ID
linear.tunnelUrlHelp: URL output after running setup-tunnel.sh in the ZIP
linear.webhookPlaceholderSet: Set - enter a new value to change
linear.saveSuccess_webhook: Saved. Linear Webhook has been automatically registered.

guide.tunnelTitle: How do I get a tunnel URL?
guide.tunnelDesc: The tunnel URL is an external address that makes your local webhook server accessible from the internet. It is automatically configured when you run bash start.sh.
guide.tunnelStep1Title: Run launcher after unzipping (tunnel & webhook auto-configured)
guide.tunnelStep2Paste: Paste the output URL into the input field below.
guide.tunnelStep3Title: When URL refresh is needed (after WSL2 restart)
guide.tunnelStep3Note: Re-running will automatically re-register the webhook with the new URL.
guide.tunnelWarning: Free Cloudflare tunnels change URL on WSL2 restart. Run bash start.sh again after restart. Registering as a systemd service (install-service.sh) keeps the webhook server and watcher running automatically.
guide.webhookDesc: A random string used for signature verification when Linear sends events to your local server. Optional but recommended for security.
guide.webhookGenerateTitle: Generate a random secret in terminal:
guide.webhookPasteDesc: Copy the output and enter it in both the input field below and WEBHOOK_SECRET in the ZIP's .env file.
guide.webhookNote: When you register a tunnel URL, ClickEye automatically registers a Linear webhook. The secret you enter here is applied to that webhook.

anthropic.title: Anthropic Credentials
anthropic.subtitle: Register the credentials required for your Claude authentication method. The appropriate credentials for the mode selected in the wizard will be automatically injected when generating a ZIP.
anthropic.apiKeyCardDescription: Used when 'API Key' mode is selected in the wizard. It is automatically injected into the ZIP's .env as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
anthropic.apiKeyInvalid: Invalid format (must start with sk-ant-... and be at least 20 characters)
anthropic.apiKeyExternalLink: Issue from console.anthropic.com → API Keys (sk-ant-... format)
anthropic.apiKeyHelper: The key is stored on the server with Fernet encryption. When analyzing solution blueprints in the wizard, this key is used first and credits from your own account are consumed before the server key.
anthropic.oauthNoRegistrationDesc: When 'OAuth Browser' mode is selected in the wizard, no credentials are stored in the ClickEye cloud.
anthropic.oauthBullet1: When you run bash start.sh, claude login proceeds automatically.
anthropic.oauthBullet2: The auth token is only stored in the OS keychain (~/.claude.json) and is not sent to ClickEye.
anthropic.oauthBullet3: There is nothing to manage on this page for this mode. Select your auth method in the wizard and download the ZIP.

guide.title: User Guide
guide.subtitle: Step-by-step guide to using the ClickEye platform.
